source: Invest Like the Best — The cyclone under the surface (EP.260)
summary: SMB-focused application software is relatively shielded from hyperscaler build-your-own risk
summary_extended: Baker argues that SMB-focused application software companies are structurally insulated from the 'build-your-own' threat that hyperscalers like AWS pose to enterprise software. His reasoning is that large enterprises have the engineering talent and resources to assemble bespoke applications using cloud primitives (e.g., AWS building blocks), effectively bypassing traditional software vendors, but SMBs lack this capability entirely. Because SMBs cannot roll their own apps on AWS, the vendors serving them face a structurally lower risk of disintermediation compared to vendors serving large enterprises. Baker frames this as a relative preference within application software — he has a directional bias toward SMB-focused software companies — though he does not name specific tickers. No quantitative targets or timeframes are cited; this is a qualitative structural view on where to concentrate within the application software landscape.
detail: Baker notes that SMBs cannot roll their own apps on AWS, making SMB-focused application software companies structurally protected from the threat that large enterprises will build their own applications using cloud primitives. This is a relative preference within application software.
confidence_evidence: Baker states this as a directional bias ('I have a bias towards ones that serve SMBs') without naming specific companies.
quote_fragment: SMBs are not going to be able to roll their own apps in AWS
